answers: Microservices = application as collection of small, independent services. Each handles one business function and communicates via APIs.
Microservices vs monolith: monolith is one big app, easy to start. Microservices are independent, scalable, but more complex to manage.
Benefits: independent deployment, technology flexibility, team autonomy, fault isolation. Challenges: complexity, network latency.
Key patterns: API Gateway, service discovery, circuit breaker, event-driven communication, and containerization with Docker/Kubernetes.
